revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Jaketsuzan Kofun]][[ja:蛇穴山古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Maebashi, Kantō, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Jaketsuzan Kofun | native_name =蛇穴山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Maebashi Zyaketuzan Kohun 1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Jaketsuzan Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Jaketsuzan kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.406806|N|139.039806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi, Gunma]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Jaketsuzan kofun'''|蛇穴山古墳|}} is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-shaped|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (''hōfun'' (方墳)) [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the Sōja neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190362|title=蛇穴山古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Jaketsuzan Kofun is located in the northwestern part of the city, about 600 meters to the west of Otobashi Bridge over the {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|qid=Q177452|lt=Tone River|1=Tone River}}. It is located in an area with a heavy concentration of ''kofun'' Kofuns.
*Overall length: 39 meters *Overall Width: 39 meters *Height: 5 meters
The tumulus is built as a {{ill|Step pyramid|en|Step pyramid|qid=Q1456099|lt=Step pyramid|1=Step pyramid}} in two tiers and there is a trace of a 12 meter wide double moat. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' nor ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a stone-lined chamber with a depth of thee meters and width of 2.6 meters and height of 1.8 meters. It contains a sandstone block which may have been a stand for the sarcophagus. The ceiling, back wall and side sides are each made with a single monolithic {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|qid=Q181908|lt=Andesite|1=Andesite}} boulders which are slightly water polished. The entrance stone is carved to resemble gate pillars. There are also traces of a plaster coating on the inside of the chamber. The burial chamber has stood open since ancient times, so no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been found.
The tumulus is believed to have been one of the last built in this area, from the end of the 7th century to the beginning of the 8th century. It is located near the {{ill|Sannō temple ruins|en|Sannō temple ruins|qid=Q44393658|lt=Sannō temple ruins|1=Sannō temple ruins}}, the {{ill|Kōzuke Kokubun-ji|en|Kōzuke Kokubun-ji|qid=Q17128356|lt=Kōzuke Kokubun-ji|1=Kōzuke Kokubun-ji}} and the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|qid=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province|1=Kōzuke Province}}.
The tumulus is located approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Sōja Station|en|Sōja Station|qid=Q844657|lt=Sōja Station|1=Sōja Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Jōetsu Line|en|Jōetsu Line|qid=Q1135061|lt=Jōetsu Line|1=Jōetsu Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|qid=Q16248234|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.maebashi-cvb.com/spot/1032 Maebashi City official site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100156418.pdf Gunma Prefectural Site] {{in lang|ja}}
Janakayama Kofun
{{Kofun navbar}}
